nodes.context: Street gang active in England from 1910s to 1930s, involved in racecourse protection and bookmaking.
Italian gang based in London, provided protection for Jewish bookmakers and rivaled the Birmingham Boys.
Smaller London gang allied with the Birmingham Boys.
Smaller London gang allied with the Birmingham Boys.

edges.context: The Birmingham Boys lost control of South East racecourses to the Sabini Gang and engaged in violent conflict.
The Birmingham Boys formed an alliance with the Hoxton Gang to control racecourses.
The Birmingham Boys formed an alliance with the Elephant and Castle Mob to control racecourses.
